title: Autocomplete Search
description:
Build an **Autocomplete** text input with a filterable dropdown of suggestions.

### Requirements

- Render a text input that filters a provided list of `suggestions` as the user types
- The dropdown should appear only when the input has **focus** and there is at least **1 matching result**
- Matching should be **case-insensitive** and check if the suggestion **includes** the query
- Clicking a suggestion fills the input with that value and **closes** the dropdown
- Pressing **Escape** closes the dropdown without selecting
- If the input is empty and focused, show **all** suggestions
- Each suggestion item should highlight the matching substring with a `<mark>` tag
- When no results match, show a "No results" message

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Text input | `ac-input` |
| Dropdown list | `ac-dropdown` |
| Suggestion item N | `ac-option-{index}` (0-indexed) |
| No-results message | `ac-no-results` |
constraints:
- Use React functional components with hooks
- Do not use any external autocomplete or combobox libraries
- Filtering must happen on every keystroke (no debouncing required)
hints: Use a combination of useState for the query and a filtered list derived from it.
Track whether the dropdown is open with a separate boolean state.
Use onFocus to open and onBlur to close — but onBlur fires before onClick on suggestions. Use onMouseDown with preventDefault to prevent the blur.
String.prototype.includes() with .toLowerCase() gives you case-insensitive matching.
